2606.06443.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
2606.06443.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly used to simulate social media users and infer how individuals may respond to online discussions. However, it remains unclear whether these simulations reflect precise user-specific beliefs or whether they are highly sensitive to semantically independent changes in conversational contexts. In this work, we study counterfactual context revision as a framework for auditing LLM-based stance simulation. Given an original online conversation, we first infer a target user's stance toward a specific topic. We then apply controlled revision strategies to the conversational context and simulate the user's stance again under the revised context. We compare text-only revision strategies with a multimodal one that incorporates meme-based context and evaluate two main effectiveness metrics, i.e., average directional stance shift and stance transition rate. The results reveal effective and robust stance transitions in both text-only and multimodal strategies across different polarization-preference mechanisms. Our study contributes an evaluation framework for understanding the context sensitivity of LLM-based stance simulation. More broadly, it highlights both the promise and risk of using LLMs to simulate online opinion dynamics.
2606.06443.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用來模擬社交媒體用戶並推斷個體可能如何對在線討論作出反應。<lb/>然而，這些模擬是否反映了精確的用戶特定信念，或者是否對語義獨立的對話上下文變化高度敏感，仍然不清楚。<lb/>在本研究中，我們研究了反事實上下文修訂作為審核基於LLM的立場模擬的框架。<lb/>給定一個原始的在線對話，我們首先推斷目標用戶對特定主題的立場。<lb/>然後，我們對對話上下文應用控制修訂策略，並在修訂後的上下文中再次模擬用戶的立場。<lb/>我們比較了僅文本的修訂策略和一種結合基於迷因的上下文的多模態策略，並評估了兩個主要的有效性指標，即平均方向立場轉變和立場轉變率。<lb/>結果顯示，在不同的極化偏好機制下，文本僅和多模態策略均顯示出有效且穩健的立場轉變。<lb/>我們的研究為理解基於LLM的立場模擬的上下文敏感性提供了一個評估框架。<lb/>更廣泛地說，它突顯了使用LLM模擬在線意見動態的前景和風險。
2606.06443.author: Xinnong Zhang et.al.
2606.06443.authors: Xinnong Zhang, Wanting Shan, Hanjia Lyu, Zhongyu Wei, Jiebo Luo
